abstract: OBJECTIVE: To evaluate the efficacy of Drug X in diabetic patients. METHODS: We conducted a double-blind RCT on 200 human adults. RESULTS: Daily administration of 500mg Drug X for 6 months reduced HbA1c levels compared to placebo. No significant differences were observed in cardiovascular events. DISCUSSION: Drug X is effective for glycemic control.

claims.text: Daily administration of Drug X reduces HbA1c levels in diabetic patients.
claims.context: 500mg daily for 6 months compared to placebo
claims.quote_anchor: Daily administration of 500mg Drug X for 6 months reduced HbA1c levels

claims.text: Drug X does not affect the risk of cardiovascular events in diabetic patients.
claims.context: 500mg daily for 6 months compared to placebo
claims.quote_anchor: No significant differences were observed in cardiovascular events.

abstract: BACKGROUND: Coffee consumption habits. METHODS: We followed a cohort of 50,000 adult women for 10 years. RESULTS: High coffee intake (>=4 cups/day) was associated with a 30% reduction in type 2 diabetes risk. Decaffeinated coffee also showed a similar trend. CONCLUSION: Coffee may prevent diabetes.

claims.text: High coffee intake is associated with a reduced risk of type 2 diabetes.
claims.quote_anchor: High coffee intake (>=4 cups/day) was associated with a 30% reduction in type 2 diabetes risk

claims.text: Decaffeinated coffee consumption is associated with a reduced risk of type 2 diabetes.
claims.quote_anchor: Decaffeinated coffee also showed a similar trend

abstract: INTRODUCTION: Metformin acts on cell growth. METHODS: We treated HeLa cells with 10mM metformin for 24 hours. RESULTS: Metformin treatment induced phosphorylation of AMPK. Furthermore, AMPK activation led to the downregulation of mTOR signaling, suppressing cell proliferation. CONCLUSION: Metformin acts via the AMPK/mTOR axis.

claims.text: Metformin treatment induces phosphorylation of AMPK.
claims.quote_anchor: Metformin treatment induced phosphorylation of AMPK

claims.text: AMPK activation leads to the downregulation of mTOR signaling.
claims.quote_anchor: AMPK activation led to the downregulation of mTOR signaling
